description.task: Write a Java program that takes a numeric score and prints the corresponding letter grade using if-else-if logic.
instructions: Use the score variable already declared as 82.
Use an if-else-if chain to assign the correct grade letter to 'grade'.
Print both lines in the exact format shown.
description.hint: Use >= and <= operators to check ranges: if (score >= 80 && score <= 89) sets grade = "B".

description.task: Write a Java program that maps an integer (1–7) to its day name using a switch-case statement.
instructions: Use a switch statement on the 'day' variable.
Map 1 → Monday, 2 → Tuesday, 3 → Wednesday, 4 → Thursday, 5 → Friday, 6 → Saturday, 7 → Sunday.
Add a default case that sets dayName to 'Invalid day'.
Print the output in the exact format shown.
description.hint: Each case must end with a break statement to prevent fall-through. The default case does not need break.

description.task: Write a Java program that checks whether a given year is a leap year using nested if-else logic.
description.context: A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 4, except for century years which must also be divisible by 400.
instructions: A year is a leap year if: (divisible by 4 AND not divisible by 100) OR (divisible by 400).
Use the modulo operator (%) to check divisibility.
Assign the boolean result to 'isLeap' before the if-else block.
Test with year = 2024 (should be a leap year) and year = 1900 (should NOT be).
description.hint: The compound condition is: isLeap = (year % 4 == 0 && year % 100 != 0) || (year % 400 == 0);
